scope_note: Per CLAUDE.md Rule 28 (D1=B partitioning). Example_* materialization is language-specific: Go uses Example_* testable functions; Python uses Examples: blocks / runnable doctests. Agents read this file only on Python-targeted runs.
note: First Python run — SEED MODE. Per-package branch coverage + doctest count baselines. New packages default to 90% branch floor (CLAUDE.md Rule #14). Doctest count is raise-only with ≥2-prior-runs precondition; first run just SEEDs the count. Established from runs/sdk-resourcepool-py-pilot-v1/impl/phase-summary.md and testing-lead T1 wave verdict.

motadata_py_sdk.resourcepool.source: runs/sdk-resourcepool-py-pilot-v1/impl/phase-summary.md (M3-M6 verified) + testing-lead T1 wave (62/62 tests pass, gate=90%)

motadata_py_sdk.resourcepool.computation_note: PEP 257 docstring + Examples: blocks on 6 of 9 public symbols (Pool, PoolConfig, PoolStats, AcquiredResource — class docstring; Pool.acquire — Examples block; module-level USAGE.md doctest). Sentinel exception classes (PoolError, PoolClosedError, PoolEmptyError, ConfigError, ResourceCreationError) intentionally have no Examples blocks. Raise-only with ≥2-prior-runs precondition per CLAUDE.md Rule 28.

raise_policy.description: Raise baseline only if current coverage exceeds baseline by >5%. Keep baseline on regression. Never lower without learning-engine reset authorization. Doctest count is raise-only; drop emits WARN only when ≥2 prior runs exist.
